expectations.text: Output performs web research on Gymshark (scrapes site or uses search) to ground the brief in real info
expectations.evidence: WebFetch used. Brief mentions specific tech stack (Next.js, Contentful, Shopify, Dynamic Yield, mParticle) and real company details.

expectations.text: Brief includes specific details about Gymshark identifying them as a large, enterprise-scale brand
expectations.evidence: Mentions multi-currency, 25+ international markets, physical retail locations, hundreds of SKUs, full tech stack — clearly identifies them as large.

expectations.text: Output explicitly flags the size mismatch as a watch-out — Gymshark is not a typical small-agency prospect
expectations.evidence: Final paragraph: 'This could be a big client but also a misfit — enterprise brands have procurement processes, slow payment cycles, and high expectations.' Size mismatch flagged, though less prominent than with_skill version.

expectations.text: Output includes a NexusPoint Positioning section with a specific angle for this prospect
expectations.evidence: No 'NexusPoint Positioning' section exists. Has 'Potential scope' bullets but no best angle statement, no service mapping, no framing of how to pitch NexusPoint to Gymshark specifically.
